main_event: Civilian technician at Sandia Corporation observed circular white object with no discernible features traveling extremely fast for 7-8 seconds from Sandia Mountains near Albuquerque, New Mexico at 1840Z on 13 October 1954.
sighted_object: circular; white; approximately one-third size of clear white circle [held at arm's length?]; edges fuzzy or blurred; no tail or exhaust; no sound; traveling extremely fast; disappeared in approximately 7-8 seconds; brought to attention by its brilliance
witness_description: Civilian [REDACTED], technical technician at Sandia Corporation, Albuquerque, NM; observed from Sandia Mountains
interesting_points: Two Air Intelligence Information Reports filed (dated 20 Oct and 2 Nov 1954) for same 13 Oct sighting
Preparing officer conclusion: reflection of sun off conventional aircraft; no a/c in area at time per operations check
Approving officer: 'indicates the probability of reflections from the sun' with additional aircraft creating afterimage against bright 50-mile-visibility sky
Conditions: bright daylight, clear sky, ceiling unlimited, visibility 50 miles — conducive to sun reflection artifacts
No radar contact; no interception attempted; no physical evidence
CSV flag G:form='one'_vs_obj='three bright glowing objects' was complete haiku hallucination; form clearly states one circular white object
CSV flag C:dur_meta=900.0s_vs_form=3.0s was haiku error; form states '7-8 seconds' (not 3 seconds or 900 seconds)
